summary: SKBitmap.Decode and SKCodec.Create return null for a specific JPEG image that is valid and renders correctly in other viewers. Tested on SkiaSharp 2.80.4 and 2.88.3 on both Linux and Windows.

stepsToReproduce: Download the attached image.zip containing the problematic JPEG

attachments.description: ZIP containing the problematic JPEG image

reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Linux Debian 11 (bullseye-slim docker) and Windows 10 (10.0.19045), x64

bugSignals.errorMessage: SKBitmap.Decode returns null; SKCodec.Create returns null

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The issue was reported on 2.80.4 and 2.88.3. It has not been confirmed whether SkiaSharp 3.x with updated Skia can decode this specific image.

analysis.summary: Skia's native codec fails to parse a specific JPEG image, causing SKCodec.Create to return null, which propagates as null from SKBitmap.Decode. The image is valid per other decoders. This is part of a broader pattern of image-specific decode failures (see #2359, #2347, #1621). Comment 2 in the thread is a separate issue related to MemoryStream position after CopyToAsync in Blazor.
analysis.rationale: The OP provides a reproducible case with an attached image, stack traces pointing to null returns from native codec creation. The image being valid in other viewers confirms this is a Skia codec limitation, not a corrupt file. Classified as type/bug since a valid image cannot be decoded. The area is area/SkiaSharp as the C# API returns null without diagnostics, though the root cause is in the native Skia codec.

keySignals.text: Cannot create Bitmap or Codec of a specific jpg image (attached). The image is not corrupted, it can be opened in Windows Photos and other image viewers
keySignals.interpretation: Confirms this is a Skia codec limitation for a specific JPEG format, not a corrupt file.

keySignals.text: Looks similar to #1621 and #1551. However, this workaround does not work
keySignals.interpretation: OP tried the SKData workaround from #1621 but it also fails, confirming the issue is in the native codec, not the stream wrapping layer.

keySignals.text: It is kind of weird to return null to indicate errors
keySignals.interpretation: Valid UX concern — SKBitmap.Decode returning null with no error info makes debugging difficult.

codeInvestigation.finding: SKBitmap.Decode(Stream) delegates to SKCodec.Create(stream) and returns null if codec is null. No error information is propagated.
SKCodec.Create(SKStream) calls sk_codec_new_from_stream which returns null if Skia cannot parse the image. The SKCodecResult out parameter is available but not surfaced in the stream overload.
C API delegates to SkCodec::MakeFromStream and SkCodec::MakeFromData. If the native codec cannot identify or parse the image format, null is returned.
WrapManagedStream creates SKManagedStream (seekable) or SKFrontBufferedManagedStream (non-seekable). This is not the cause since the OP also tried SKData-based decode.

workarounds: Pre-process the problematic JPEG with another library (e.g., System.Drawing, ImageSharp, or ImageMagick) to re-encode as PNG before passing to SkiaSharp
Use SKCodec.Create with the out SKCodecResult parameter to get a diagnostic error code
nextQuestions: What JPEG profile/features does the attached image use? (CMYK, unusual ICC profile, non-standard markers)
Does SkiaSharp 3.x with an updated Skia version decode this image successfully?
Is the issue in libjpeg-turbo (used by Skia) or in Skia's codec layer above it?

resolution.hypothesis: The specific JPEG image uses a format feature or profile that Skia's native codec (via libjpeg-turbo) does not support. Since the image renders in other viewers, this is a codec coverage gap rather than a corrupt file.

proposals.title: Pre-process image with alternative codec
proposals.description: Use System.Drawing.Common, SixLabors.ImageSharp, or ImageMagick to load the problematic JPEG and re-encode it as PNG before passing to SkiaSharp.

proposals.title: Use SKCodecResult for diagnostics
proposals.description: Use the SKCodec.Create overload with out SKCodecResult to get the specific failure reason (e.g., InvalidInput, IncompleteInput, Unimplemented).

proposals.title: Test with latest SkiaSharp 3.x
proposals.description: SkiaSharp 3.x includes a significantly updated Skia engine. The JPEG codec may have been improved to handle this image format.

resolution.recommendedReason: This is the fastest path to understanding the root cause. The SKCodecResult will reveal what specifically Skia's codec doesn't like about this image.

actionability.reason: Reproducible bug with attached image. Need to download the image, examine its JPEG profile, and test with latest SkiaSharp to determine if this is still an issue or was fixed by Skia updates.

actions.description: Apply bug, SkiaSharp, and platform labels
Post analysis with diagnostics suggestion and workaround
